name: seedance-plugin
description: Generate cinematic AI video clips (5-20s) using ByteDance Seedance 2.0 via OpenRouter. Supports text-to-video, first/last-frame image conditioning, native audio-video joint generation, and multimodal reference inputs (up to 9 reference images, 3 reference videos, 3 reference audio clips).
author: AGNT

schema.title: Seedance 2.0 Video
schema.description: Generate 5-20s cinematic AI video clips using ByteDance Seedance 2.0 via OpenRouter. Pair with the Remotion plugin to add branded motion graphics, logos, and captions. v1.1 adds multimodal reference inputs: up to 9 reference images, 3 reference videos, and 3 reference audio clips for style/motion/rhythm continuity across scenes.

prompt.description: Cinematic shot description. Formula: [camera] [subject] [action] [environment] [lighting] [style]. When references are supplied, address them with [Image1]/[Video1]/[Audio1] or @Image1/@Video1/@Audio1. Example: 'Slow dolly-in on a chrome robot hand assembling a glowing cube. Use [Video1] for camera rhythm. Use [Audio1] for percussion. Use [Image1] for color palette.'

aspectRatio.description: 9:16 for Reels/Shorts, 16:9 for YouTube, 1:1 for feed posts

useFastTier.description: Use Seedance 2.0 Fast model (cheaper, lower fidelity)

generateAudio.description: Enable Seedance 2.0 native audio-video joint generation (ambient sound and effects)

firstFrameUrl.description: Optional: URL of first-frame reference image (image-to-video mode). Combine with referenceVideoUrls/referenceAudioUrls for continuity + style memory.

lastFrameUrl.description: Optional: URL of last-frame reference image

referenceImageUrls.description: Optional: newline- or comma-separated public image URLs to use as style/composition references. Up to 9. Address them in the prompt as [Image1], [Image2], ... or @Image1, @Image2, ...

referenceVideoUrls.description: Optional: newline- or comma-separated public MP4/MOV URLs (e.g. prior Seedance clips) to use as motion/camera-rhythm references. Up to 3. Address them as [Video1], [Video2], ... or @Video1, @Video2, ...

referenceAudioUrls.description: Optional: newline- or comma-separated public MP3/WAV URLs to use as rhythm/sound-design references. Up to 3. Cannot be used alone — pair with at least one image, video, first frame, or last frame reference. Address them as [Audio1], [Audio2], ... or @Audio1, @Audio2, ...

referencePayloadMode.description: Advanced: payload shape used to submit references. 'auto' tries openrouter-normalized first, then provider-lists if the provider rejects unknown reference fields (only retries on pre-job validation errors). 'raw-only' sends only core params plus rawPassthroughJson.

rawPassthroughJson.description: Advanced: a JSON object merged into the OpenRouter request body AFTER reference fields. Wins on key conflicts. Use to test new provider-specific fields without rebuilding the plugin. Must be a JSON object (not an array or scalar).

seed.description: Optional: reproducibility seed (passed through if accepted by provider).

includeDebugRequest.description: Advanced: return a redacted summary of the actual request body (auth removed, signed URLs shortened) for debugging unknown-field errors.

filename.description: Optional: Override output filename (auto-generated UUID if omitted)

filePath.description: Absolute path to the downloaded MP4 file (feeds into remotion-compose)

cost.description: Generation cost in USD if reported by provider

contentUrl.description: Provider content URL when reported in the completed job payload (typically signed; may expire). Suitable for immediate chained reference-video reuse.

unsignedUrl.description: First unsigned/public provider URL when reported. Often the cleanest URL to feed back into the next call's referenceVideoUrls.

pollUrl.description: Polling URL used for the job (debugging / advanced reuse).

referenceSummary.description: Counts and URL basenames for the references that were actually submitted, plus any prompt-mention warnings.

requestShape.description: Payload mode used for the submitted request: openrouter-normalized | provider-lists | content-array | raw-only.

debugRequest.description: Redacted request body — only populated when includeDebugRequest=Yes. Auth headers removed, signed URLs shortened.
